architecture.summary: The system analyzes and controls emotion in LLM-based TTS models by training sparse autoencoders (SAEs) on semantic hidden states to identify sparse latent features associated with emotion. A feature-level intervention framework then steers subsets of these sparse features to induce or suppress emotions without modifying backbone model parameters. The mechanism reads the LLM's own hidden activations, decomposes them via SAE, and writes modified activations back to control acoustic attributes like pitch.
trained_components: sparse autoencoders (SAEs) on LLM semantic hidden states

biblio.title: Sparse Autoencoders for Interpretable Emotion Control in Text-to-Speech

claims.statement: emotional variation is distributed across multiple sparse latent features
intervening on a small subset of sparse latent features enables interpretable emotion control
distinct latent features are associated with specific acoustic attributes (e.g., pitch)
emotional expression arises from coordinated latent contributions rather than a single global shift

baseline.name: global activation steering and existing TTS baselines

claims.statement: steering sparse latent features achieves comparable or superior emotion induction and suppression performance relative to global steering and existing TTS baselines
feature-level intervention framework enables bidirectional emotion induction and suppression without modifying backbone parameters
